heading: Create a Service Account for an Organization
body:
Use the following procedure to grant programmatic access to an
organization by creating a service account and generating a new
client secret. To learn more about authentication methods for the
Atlas Administration API, see [Atlas Administration API Authentication Methods](api-authentication).

- Enter a Name.

- Enter a Description.

- Select a duration from the Client Secret Expiration menu.

- From the **Organization Permissions** menu, select the
  [organization role](organization-roles) for the
  service account.

  These roles determine the permissions of the access tokens
  generated by this service account. You use access tokens to
  authenticate to the Atlas Administration API when you [Make an API (Application Programming Interface) Request](example-api-request).

  You can also assign project-level roles to the service account
  after you create it. To learn how, see [Grant Existing Service Account Access to a Project](invite-org-app-api-keys).

The client secret acts as the password when creating access tokens.

> **Warning:** *This is the only time you can view the full client secret.* Click
> Copy and save it to a secure location. Otherwise, you'll need to
> generate a new client secret.

- Click Add Access List Entry.

- Enter an IP (Internet Protocol) address or CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block from which you want Atlas
  to accept API (Application Programming Interface) requests for this service account.

  You can also click Use Current IP Address if the host
  you are using to access Atlas will also make API (Application Programming Interface) requests
  using this service account.

- Click Save.
